verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Then answered the Jews, and said unto Him, Say we not well that thou art a Samaritan, and hast a devil?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Jesus answered, I have not a devil; but I honor My Father.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: A shameless and a forward thing is wickedness, and when it ought to hide itself, then is it the fiercer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As was the case with the Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when they ought to have been pricked by what was said, admiring the boldness and conclusiveness of the words, they even insult Him, calling Him a Samaritan, and saying that He had a devil, and they ask, “Said we not well that thou art a Samaritan, and hast a devil?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because when He uttereth anything sublime, this is thought among the very senseless to be madness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet nowhere before did the Evangelist say that they called Him “a Samaritan”; but from this expression it is probable that this had been often asserted by them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Thou hast a devil,” saith some one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Who is it that hath a devil?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He that honoreth God, or he that insulteth Him that honoreth Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then saith Christ, who is very meekness and gentleness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I have not a devil, but I honor Him that sent me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Where there was need to instruct them, to pull down their excessive insolence, to teach them not to be proud because of Abraham, He was vehement; but when it was needful that He being insulted should bear it, He used much gentleness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When they said, “We have God and Abraham for our Father,” He touched them sharply; but when they called Him a demoniac, He spake submissively, thus teaching us to avenge insults offered to God, but to overlook such as are offered to ourselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I seek not Mine own glory.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “These things,” He saith, “I have spoken to show that it becometh not you, being murderers, to call God your Father; so that I have spoken them through honor for Him, and for His sake do I hear these reproaches, and for His sake do ye dishonor Me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet I care not for this insolence ; to Him, for whose sake I now hear these things, ye owe an account of your words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For ‘I seek not Mine own glory.’

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore I omit to punish you, and betake Myself to exhortation, and counsel you so to act, that ye shall not only escape punishment, but also attain eternal life.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Verily, verily, I say unto you, If a man keep My saying, he shall never see death.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here He speaketh not of faith only, but of a pure life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Above He said, “shall have everlasting life,” but here, “shall not see death.” ( c. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: At the same time He hinteth to them that they could do nothing against Him, for if the man that should keep His saying should not die, much less should He Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: At least they understood it so, and said to Him,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Now we know that thou hast a devil; Abraham is dead, and the Prophets are dead.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That is, “they who heard the word of God are dead, and shall they who have heard thine not die?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Art thou greater than our father Abraham?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Alas for their vainglory!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again do they betake themselves to his relationship.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet it would have been suitable to say, “Art thou greater than God? or they who have heard thee than Abraham?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But they say not this, because they thought that He was even less than Abraham.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: At first, therefore, He showed that they were murderers, and so led them away from the relationship; but when they persevered, He contrived this in another way, showing that they labored uselessly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And concerning the “death,” He said nothing to them, neither did He reveal or tell them what kind of death He meant, but in the meantime He would have them believe, that He is greater than Abraham, that even by this He may put them to shame.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Certainly,” He saith, “were I a common man I ought not to die, having done no wrong; but when I speak the truth, and have no sin, am sent from God, and am greater than Abraham, are ye not mad, do ye not labor in vain when ye attempt to kill Me?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then is their reply?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Now we know that thou hast a devil.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not so spake the woman of Samaria.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: She said not to Him, “Thou hast a devil”; but only, “Art thou greater than our father Jacob?” ( c. iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For these men were insolent and accursed, while she desired to learn; wherefore she doubted and answered with proper moderation, and called Him, “Lord.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For one who promised far greater things, and who was worthy of credit, ought not to have been insulted, but even admired; yet these men said that He had a devil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Those expressions of the Samaritan woman were those of one in doubt; these were the words of men unbelieving and perverse.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Art thou greater than our father Abraham?” so that this (which He had said) maketh Him to be greater than Abraham.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “When therefore ye have seen Him lifted up, ye shall confess that He is greater.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account He said, “When ye have lifted Me up, ye shall know that I Am.” ( Ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And observe His wisdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Having first rent them away from Abraham’s kindred, He showeth that He is greater than Abraham, that so He may be seen to be very exceedingly greater than the Prophets also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Indeed it was because they continually called Him a prophet that He said, “My word hath no place in you.” ( Ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In that other place He declared that He raiseth the dead, but here He saith, “He that believeth shall never see death,” which was a much greater thing than not to allow believers to be holden, by death.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore the Jews were the more enraged.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then say they?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Whom makest thou thyself?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this too in an insulting manner.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Thou art taking somewhat upon thyself,” saith one of them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To this then Christ replieth;

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If I honor Myself, My honor is nothing.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What say the heretics here?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That He heard the question, “Art thou greater than our father Abraham?” and dared not to say to them, “Yea, I am greater,” but did so in a covert manner.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Is His honor “nothing”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: With respect to them it is nothing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And as He said, “My witness is not true” ( c. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 31 ), with reference to the opinion they would form of it, so also doth He speak here.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “There is One that honoreth Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And wherefore said He not, “The Father that sent Me,” as He did before, but,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Of whom ye say that He is your God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Yet ye have not known Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because He desired to show that they not only knew not His Father, but that they knew not God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “So that to say, ‘I know Him,’ is not a boast, while to say, ‘I know Him not,’ would be a falsehood; but ye when ye say that ye know Him, lie; as then ye, when ye say that ye know Him, lie, so also should I, were I to say that I know Him not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If I honor Myself.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since they said, “Whom makest thou thyself?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He replieth, “If I make (Myself anything,) My honor is nothing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As then I know Him exactly, so ye know Him not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And as in the case of Abraham, He did not take away their whole assertion, but said, “I know that ye are Abraham’s seed,” so as to make the charge against them heavier; thus here He doth not remove the whole, but what?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Whom ye say.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By granting to them their boast of words, He increaseth the force of the accusation against them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then do ye “not know Him”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Because ye insult One who saith and doeth everything that He may be glorified, even when that One is sent from Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This assertion is unsupported by testimony, but what follows serves to establish it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And I keep His saying.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here they might, if at least they had anything to say, have refuted Him, for it was the strongest proof of His having been sent by God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Your father Abraham rejoiced to see My day, and he saw it, and was glad.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again, He showeth that they were aliens from the race of Abraham, if they grieved at what he rejoiced in.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “My day,” seems to me to mean the day of the Crucifixion, which Abraham foreshowed typically by the offering of the ram and of Isaac.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What do they reply?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Thou art not yet forty years old, and hast Thou seen Abraham?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So that we conclude that Christ was nearly forty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Jesus saith unto them, Before Abraham was, I Am.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then took they up stones to cast at Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou how He proved Himself to be greater than Abraham?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the man who rejoiced to see His day, and made this an object of earnest desire, plainly did so because it was a day that should be for a benefit, and belonging to one greater than himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because they had said, “The carpenter’s son” ( Matt. xiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 55 ), and imagined nothing more concerning Him, He leadeth them by degrees to an exalted notion of Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore when they heard the words, “Ye know not God,” they were not grieved; but when they heard, “before Abraham was, I Am,” as though the nobility of their descent were debased, they became furious, and would have stoned Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He saw My day, and was glad.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He showeth, that not unwillingly He came to His Passion, since He praiseth him who was gladdened at the Cross.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this was the salvation of the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But they cast stones at Him; so ready were they for murder, and they did this of their own accord, without enquiry.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But wherefore said He not, “Before Abraham was, I was,” instead of “I Am”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As the Father useth this expression, “I Am,” so also doth Christ; for it signifieth continuous Being, irrespective of all time.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On which account the expression seemed to them to be blasphemous.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now if they could not bear the comparison with Abraham, although this was but a trifling one, had He continually made Himself equal to the Father, would they ever have ceased casting stones at Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: After this, again He fleeth as a man, and concealeth Himself, having laid before them sufficient instruction: and having accomplished His work, He went forth from the Temple, and departed to heal the blind, proving by His actions that He is before Abraham.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But perhaps some one will say, “Why did He not paralyze their strength?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So they would have believed.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He healed the paralytic, yet they believed not; nay, He wrought ten thousand wonders; at the very Passion He cast them to the ground, and darkened their eyes, yet they believed not; and how would they have believed if He had paralyzed their strength?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: There is nothing worse than a soul hardened in desperation; though it see signs and wonders, it still perseveres in retaining the same shamelessness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus Pharaoh, who received ten thousand strokes, was sobered only while being punished, and continued of this character until the last day of his life, pursuing those whom he had let go.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore Paul continually saith, “Lest any of you be hardened by the deceitfulness of sin.” ( Heb. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as the callosities of the body, when formed, become dead, and possess no sensation; so the soul, when it is occupied by many passions, becomes dead to virtue; and apply what you will to it, it gets no perception of the matter, but whether you threaten punishment or anything else, continues insensible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore I beseech you, while we have hopes of salvation, while we can turn, to use every means to do so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For men who have become past feeling, are after that in the blind state of despairing pilots, who give up their vessel to the wind, and themselves contribute no assistance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus the envious man looks to one thing only, that is, to satisfy his lust, and though he be like to be punished or even slain, still he is possessed solely by that passion; and in like manner the intemperate and avaricious.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if the sovereignty of the passions be so great, much greater is that of virtue; if for them we despise death, much more for this; if they (sinners) regard not their own lives, much less ought we to do so in the cause of our salvation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For what shall we have to say, if when they who perish are so active about their own perdition, we for our own salvation manifest not even an equal activity, but ever continue wasting with envy?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nothing is worse than envy; to destroy another it destroys itself also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The eye of the envious wastes away in grief, he lives in a continual death, he deems all men, even those who have never wronged him, his enemies.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He grieves that God is honored, he rejoices in what the devil rejoices in.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Is any honored among men?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This is not honor, envy him not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But is he honored by God?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Strive and be thou like him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thou wilt not?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why then dost thou destroy thyself too?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why castest thou away what thou hast?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Canst thou not be like unto him, nor gain any good thing?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why then dost thou besides this take for thyself evil, when thou oughtest to rejoice with him, that so even if thou be not able to share his toils, thou mayest profit by rejoicing with Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For often even the will is able to effect great good.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: At least Ezekiel saith, that the Moabites were punished because they rejoiced over the Israelites, and that certain others were saved because they mourned over the misfortunes of their neighbors. ( Ezek. xxv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now if there be any comfort for those who mourn over the woes of others, much more for those who rejoice at the honors of others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He charged the Moabites with having exulted over the Israelites, yet it was God that punished them; but not even when He punisheth will He have us rejoice over those that are punished.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it is not His wish to punish them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now if we must condole with those who are punished, much more must we avoid envying those who are honored.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus, for example, Corah and Dathan perished with their company, making those whom they envied brighter, and giving themselves up to punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For a venomous beast is envy, an unclean beast, a deliberate vice which admits not of pardon, a wickedness stripped of excuse, the cause and mother of all evils.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore let us pluck it up by the roots, that we may be freed from evil here, and may obtain blessings hereafter; through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom and with whom, to the Father and the Holy Ghost, be glory now and ever and world without end.
